body:
Creates the first official release of `johansabent/antigravity-kit-jp`, a security-hardened fork of `vudovn/antigravity-kit`. Establishes fork identity and automates future releases via GitHub Actions.

## Changes

### Fork identity
- **`package.json`** — name → `antigravity-kit-jp`, version → `1.0.0`, all URLs point to the fork
- **`README.md`** — new title, fork-specific badges (release, CodeQL, Security Hardened), "What's Different" section at top; upstream attribution in footer replaces original "Buy me coffee" section
- **`SECURITY.md`** — supported versions table: `2.x` → `1.x`

### Release history
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — adds `[1.0.0]` entry documenting all fork-specific work (CWE-78/693/200/798 fixes, CI/CD workflows, Dependabot, audit docs); upstream history preserved below a separator; comparison/release links updated to fork repo

### Automated releases
- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — triggers on `v*` tag push; extracts the matching `CHANGELOG.md` section as release notes and publishes a GitHub Release via `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`

## To publish v1.0.0 after merge
```bash
git tag v1.0.0
git push origin v1.0.0
```
The workflow handles the rest.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR establishes the first official release (v1.0.0) of `johansabent/antigravity-kit-jp`, a security-hardened fork of `vudovn/antigravity-kit`. The release includes fork identity establishment, comprehensive changelog documentation, and automated GitHub release workflows. The fork inherits all upstream functionality from v2.0.2 while adding a professional security layer and CI/CD infrastructure. **Changes:** - Rebranded package identity with fork-specific naming, URLs, and version reset to 1.0.0 - Documented comprehensive changelog with security fixes (CWE-78, 693, 200, 798) and new CI/CD workflows - Automated GitHub release workflow that extracts version-specific notes from CHANGELOG.md ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | package.json | Updated name to `antigravity-kit-jp`, version to 1.0.0, all URLs to fork repository, added "security" keyword | | SECURITY.md | Updated supported versions table from 2.x to 1.x to reflect fork versioning | | README.md | Added fork branding with "JP" suffix, fork-specific badges, "What's Different" section highlighting security improvements, upstream attribution footer | | CHANGELOG.md | Added comprehensive v1.0.0 release entry documenting all security fixes and CI/CD additions, preserved upstream history with clear separator, updated version comparison links | | .github/workflows/release.yml | New workflow automating GitHub releases on version tags using changelog extraction via awk/sed |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **.github/workflows/release.yml:32** * The sed command only removes leading blank lines but not trailing ones. Consider adding a second command to remove trailing blank lines as well. You can chain it with: `sed -i -e '/./,$!d' -e :a -e '/^\n*$/{$d;N;ba' -e '}' /tmp/release_notes.md` or use a simpler approach: `sed -i -e '/./,$!d' -e :a -e '/^\n*$/{ N; s/\n$//; ta }' /tmp/release_notes.md` Alternatively, for better readability and maintenance, you could use: ```bash sed -i '/./,$!d; :a; /^\s*$/{ $d; N; ba }' /tmp/release_notes.md ``` This ensures the release notes don't have unnecessary blank lines at the end that could make the GitHub release description look unprofessional. ``` sed -i '/./,$!d' /tmp/release_notes.md ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Code Review

This pull request does a great job of establishing the fork's identity for its v1.0.0 release. The updates to `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `package.json` are clear and appropriate. The new `mcp-integration` skill and `/git` workflow are well-documented. However, there is a critical issue where comments have been added to `.json` files. Standard JSON does not support comments, and this will cause parsing errors. These comments must be removed to ensure the configuration files are valid.

title: feat: v1.0.0 release — professional fork identity, changelog, and automated releases
